title: Historic Tariff Market Crash: Dow -2,231 pts, S&P -5.97%, Nasdaq Enters Bear Market; $6.6T Wiped in Two Days
detail: April 4 saw the second catastrophic day of a historic market selloff triggered by Trump's Liberation Day anniversary tariff announcements (April 2) and China's retaliatory 34% tariff on all US goods. The Dow Jones fell 2,231 points (5.5%), the S&P 500 lost 5.97%, and the Nasdaq fell 5.8%, formally entering bear market territory (down 20%+ from its peak). Over the two-day period of April 3–4, the Dow lost over 4,000 points (9.48%), the S&P 500 fell roughly 10%, and the Nasdaq lost approximately 11% — marking the largest two-day market cap loss in history at over $6.6 trillion erased. China's Ministry of Commerce announced the 34% retaliatory tariff on April 4, effective April 10. Trump — rather than calming markets — posted a video to Truth Social on April 4 suggesting he was deliberately crashing markets to force Fed Chair Jerome Powell to lower interest rates, further unsettling investor confidence. Recession probability models surged: Moody's AI model reached 49%, Goldman Sachs raised its 12-month US recession probability. European and Asian markets also fell sharply in sympathy.

media.caption: Dow falls 2,231 points on April 4 as China retaliates with 34% tariff — historic two-day $6.6T wipeout

title: US Rescues One F-15E Crew Member From Deep Inside Iran; Second Crew Member Still Missing
detail: US Special Operations forces rescued one crew member of the F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran on April 3 during Operation Epic Fury. The rescue was described as being conducted 'deep in enemy territory.' The operation involved 21 aircraft in the first rescue wave; two Black Hawk helicopters were damaged during the extraction. The second crew member — described in reports as a colonel serving as the Weapon Systems Officer — remained missing as of April 4. The downed F-15E was the first confirmed US aircraft loss of the Iran war, occurring on Day 35 of operations. The rescue operation underlined the risks of sustained strike operations into Iranian airspace. US Central Command did not confirm or deny the details publicly, but multiple outlets — NBC News, the Washington Post, and Military.com — reported the rescue citing US officials. The missing crew member would be recovered by April 5.
